description: largest student-led civil rights organization during the American Civil Rights Movement
description_wiki: The Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, and later the Student National Coordinating Committee (SNCC, pronounced  SNIK), was the principal channel of student commitment in the United States to the civil rights movement during the 1960s.

description: American businesswoman known for civil rights activism
description_wiki: Diane Judith Nash (born May 15, 1938) is an American civil rights activist and strategist of the student wing of the Civil Rights Movement.

description: 1960s Civil Rights Movement strategist (1936–2008)
description_wiki: James Luther Bevel (October 19, 1936 – December 19, 2008) was an American minister and a leader and major strategist of the 1960s Civil Rights Movement.

description: county in Tennessee, United States
description_wiki: Fayette County is a county located in the U.S. state of Tennessee.

description: American activist (1941-1998)
description_wiki: Kwame Ture ( KWAH-may TOOR-ay; born Stokely Standiford Churchill Carmichael; June 29, 1941 – November 15, 1998) was a Trinidadian and American activist who played a major role in the civil rights movement in the United States and the global pan-African movement.

description: American civil rights activist (1935–2021)
description_wiki: Robert Parris Moses (January 23, 1935 – July 25, 2021) was an American educator and civil rights activist known for his work as a leader of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC) on voter education and registration in Mississippi during the Civil Rights Movement, and his co-founding of the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party.

description: American activist

description: American civil rights activist

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: branch of Freedom Democratic party during 1960s Civil Rights Movement
description_wiki: The Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party (MFDP), also referred to simply as the Freedom Democratic Party, was an American political party that existed in the state of Mississippi from 1964 to 1968 during the Civil Rights Movement.

description_wiki: Atlantic City, sometimes referred to by its initials A.C., is a seaside resort city in Atlantic County, in the U.S. state of New Jersey.
